function: levenshtein(s1, s2, int id, int r, int t)
sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_strings (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, str1 VARCHAR(255), str2 VARCHAR(255));
CREATE TABLE test_ips (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ip inet);
SELECT id, levenshtein(str1, str2, 4, 2, 1) AS distance FROM test_strings WHERE str1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id FROM test_ips WHERE ip >>= inet '192.168.1.0';
SELECT id, levenshtein(str1, str2, 4, 2, 1) AS distance FROM test_strings WHERE levenshtein(str1, str2, 4, 2, 1) > 0 HAVING distance < 5;
SELECT a.id, b.ip FROM test_strings a JOIN test_ips b ON levenshtein(a.str1, a.str2, 4, 2, 1) > 0 WHERE b.ip >>= inet '192.168.1.10' AND a.str1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT *, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings) AS total_strings, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_ips) AS total_ips FROM test_ips WHERE ip IS NOT NULL AND ip >>= inet '192.168.1.0' HAVING total_strings > 0;
SELECT str1, str2 FROM test_strings WHERE (levenshtein(str1, str2, 4, 2, 1) IS NOT NULL) AND (str1 <> '' OR str2 <> '');
